nameMeanings.name: Oglethorpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Oglethorpe Hall in Bramham (WR Yorks), which is recorded as Ocelestorp in 1086 and Okelesthorp in 1242. The place-name derives from the Old Scandinavian personal name Oddkell + Old Scandinavian or Old English þorp ‘secondary settlement, outlying farmstead’.

nameMeanings.name: Owen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern form of the Welsh name Owain or a transferred use of the surname derived from the personal name. It is also used as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Eóghan (see Scottish and Irish appendices), which is probably ultimately a cognate of the Welsh name. Although retaining its Welsh flavour, this is one of a number of Celtic names which have recently come into general use and have become increasingly popular since the 1990s.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: Sir Owen John Oglethorpe was born in 1440, in Bramham, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 2 sons with Jane Hayward. He died in 1500, in his hometown, at the age of 60.
